data.title: Kaiser Aluminum Files 8K - Other Events >KALU
data.description:
Kaiser Aluminum Corp. (KALU) filed a Form 8K - Other Events - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on January 13, 2026.

On January 13, 2026, Kaiser Aluminum Corporation announced that its board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend on its common stock of $0.77 per share. The dividend will be payable on February 13, 2026 to stockholders of record as of the close of business on January 23, 2026. A copy of the press release is attached hereto as Exhibit 99.1.

The information contained in the press release shall not be deemed to be "filed" for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ("Exchange Act"), or otherwise subject to liability of that section. In addition, this information shall not be incorporated by reference into any registration statement or other document filed under the Securities Act, or the Exchange Act, regardless of any general incorporation language in such filing, except as shall be expressly set forth by specific reference in the filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/811596/000119312526011824/kalu-20260113.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/811596/000119312526011824/0001193125-26-011824-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Corn, soybeans hit lowest prices since October on big supplies
data.description:
Key points:
  • Corn hits lowest price since late October on bigger-than-expected US supply outlook
  • Soybeans weak after USDA cuts export forecast, raises Brazil's harvest view

By Karl Plume

U.S. corn futures slid to a three-month low on Tuesday, extending declines from the prior day triggered by the U.S. Department of Agriculture's outlook for massive supplies following a record-large U.S. harvest.

Soybeans touched a two-and-a-half-month low after the USDA on Monday trimmed its U.S. export view and raised its harvest estimate for rival supplier Brazil, while wheat futures followed corn and soybeans lower.

The USDA caught the corn market wrong-footed by increasing its estimate of the 2025 U.S. harvest to a new record, contrary to expectations of a downward revision, while also pegging U.S. quarterly stocks of the cereal at their largest ever.

Losses on Tuesday were tempered by bargain-buying and technical support, and as traders turned their focus to weather in Brazil, where farmers will soon be planting their large second corn crop.

"For corn, this was a rip-the-Band-Aid-off event and these are probably the worst numbers we're going to see. If anything, there is some risk on South American weather," said Ted Seifried, chief market strategist at Zaner Group.

"But for soybeans, I don't feel like it's the same sort of mentality," he said.

Brazil's record-large harvest is likely to undercut demand for U.S. soy in the coming months, and it remains unclear if a recent surge in U.S. purchases by China will continue after a 12-million-metric-ton buying target is achieved.

Chicago Board of Trade March corn (CH26) fell 1-3/4 cents to $4.19-3/4 a bushel after earlier touching the lowest point for a most-active contract since October 16. March soybeans (SH26) were down 10-1/4 cents at $10.38-3/4 a bushel, the lowest price since October 23. CBOT March wheat (WH26) was down 3/4 cent at $5.10-1/2 a bushel.

data.title: WeatherTech founder tapped for Federal Trade Commission
data.description:
WeatherTech founder David MacNeil has been nominated for a seat on the U.S. Federal Trade Commission, FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson said in a post on social media site X on Tuesday.

"I look forward to working with this outstanding businessman and great patriot," Ferguson said.

President Donald Trump had previously nominated former Texas attorney Ryan Baasch to an open commissioner slot. But Trump opted instead to give Baasch a role on the National Economic Council, a White House official told Reuters last week.

The FTC is structured so that no more than three of the five commissioners come from the same party. Ferguson and commissioner Mark Meador are Republicans.

Trump fired the agency's two Democratic commissioners in March, sparking a U.S. Supreme Court case that could tighten the president's control over government agencies designed by Congress to be somewhat insulated from politics.

data.title: Top News Today: Stocks Fall After JPMorgan Earnings, Inflation Data
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

STOCKS: Stocks fell after lackluster earnings from JPMorgan Chase.

TREASURYS: Treasury yields fell after steady consumer inflation data.

FOREX: The U.S. dollar rose against peers as traders weighed the probability that Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell would be ousted before his term ends.

COMMODITIES: Crude oil futures settled at their highest level since late October after President Trump imposed tariffs on countries that do business with Iran and urged Iranians to continue protesting, saying on social media that help was on its way.

HEADLINES

Pace of Inflation Held Steady in December; Consumer Prices Up 2.7% on Year

Inflation remained relatively contained at the close of 2025 despite turbulence from tariffs, giving the Federal Reserve some breathing room as it weighs the threat of price pressures against a cooling labor market.

Consumer prices rose 2.7% in December from a year earlier, the Labor Department said Tuesday. That was unchanged from November's pace and in line with the expectations of economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal.

The consumer-price index report showed the impact of tariffs is still relatively muted. Last spring, a big fear was that Trump's new tariffs would fuel massive price increases, but some companies have been eating the cost or raising prices only slowly. Tuesday's report also offers a measure of relief to Fed officials who lowered rates last month and have signaled they plan to hold them steady for now.

Jamie Dimon Warns Interfering With Fed Would Push Up Rates

Jamie Dimon on Tuesday defended the Federal Reserve after it was subpoenaed by the Justice Department, saying that "anything that chips away" at the central bank's independence "is not a good idea."

Dimon, speaking with reporters after JPMorgan Chase issued its fourth-quarter earnings results, said political interference with the Fed would cause inflation and interest rates to go up, contrary to President Trump's stated goal of getting rates to go lower. Dimon's remarks came after Fed Chair Jerome Powell disclosed this weekend that he was being investigated by the Justice Department.

The 69 year-old chief executive and de-facto leader of the nation's finance executives has been defending Powell and the Fed from attempts at political interference for months, both publicly and privately to the president.

JPMorgan Profit Falls on Investment-Banking Miss, Apple Card Charge

JPMorgan Chase reported that the bank's profit fell 7% in the fourth quarter of 2025, dragged down by a charge from its deal to take over the Apple credit card program and a surprising slip in investment banking fees.

Still, the nation's biggest bank saw revenue increase for both the quarter and the full-year and was optimistic about the trajectory of the U.S. economy and consumer.

"Consumers have money, there's still jobs even though it's weakened a little bit," Chief Executive Jamie Dimon said on a call with analysts.

U.S. New Home Sales Pick Up After Summer, Delayed Data Say

The rate of sales of new homes was relatively flat between September and October last year, but up from the summer, as the Federal Reserve rate-cutting cycle started to pick up. Here are the main takeaways from the delayed report released by the Census Bureau and the Department of Housing and Urban Development Tuesday:

-Sales of new single-family homes edged down to 737,000 in October last year, from 738,000 in September, but up from a downwardly revised 711,000 in August.

-A consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected 718,000 sales in October.

Delta Expects Premium Fliers to Boost Its Profit This Year

Delta Air Lines expects high-earning fliers who shell out for premium amenities to boost its profit this year.

The biggest U.S. airline by market capitalization wants to increase its selection of seats in first class and Delta One, its business-class cabin on international flights with lie-flat seats.

"In the coming year, effectively none of our growth in seats will be in the main cabin," Delta Chief Executive Ed Bastian said. "Virtually all will be in the premium sector."

Boeing Shipped 63 Jets in December, 600 in 2025

Boeing delivered 63 airplanes in December, bringing the airplane manufacturer's 2025 total to 600 jets and giving it the busiest shipping year since 2018.

The company said Tuesday that it shipped 18 widebody jets and 45 737s in December, with the largest orders going to Vietjet Air, TUI, American, United and Southwest.

The company also reported 174 net orders for the month, after adjusting for cancellations and jets taken out of reserve. The largest buyer by far was Alaska Airlines, which announced last week that it would buy 105 737 MAX jets and five 787 Dreamliners.

BNY Profit Rises, CEO Calls Pressure on Fed 'Counterproductive'

Bank of New York Mellon's chief executive criticized the Trump administration's pressure on the Federal Reserve in a media call Tuesday that came after the company reported record revenue for 2025.

Chief Executive Robin Vince said that the pressure on the Fed is "counterproductive" to the administration's goal of improving affordability. The independence of the Fed is a critical underpin of the bond market, "so shaking at the foundation of it doesn't seem to be to us accomplishing the administration's primary objectives," Vince said.

Threatening that independence could push up interest rates, increasing the cost of borrowing for Americans "because the market potentially has to worry about something that frankly, they shouldn't have to worry about," he said.

Pentagon Investing $1 Billion in L3Harris's Rocket Motor Business

The Pentagon said it would invest $1 billion in L3Harris Technologies' missile business to turbocharge production of weapons that officials want on hand for future conflicts.

The defense contractor said Tuesday it plans to spin off the missile segment into a separate publicly traded company, backed by the Pentagon's minority investment. The standalone business would focus on making rocket parts for the U.S. and its allies, including those for Patriot, Thaad and Tomahawk missiles.

L3Harris shares were down slightly on Tuesday afternoon, in line with major indexes.

U.S. Small-Business Confidence Rises Amid Brighter Outlook Expectations

Small-business confidence in the U.S. rose in December, driven by a jump in those expecting better business conditions going forward.

The National Federation of Independent Business said Tuesday that its small-business optimism index rose to 99.5, compared with 99.0 in November. The figure remains above the index's long-term average of 98, and came in above a consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal that expected the index to remain unchanged.he NFIB survey provides a monthly snapshot of small businesses in the U.S., which account for nearly half of private-sector jobs.

Global Central Bankers Rush to Defend Fed's Powell

Top global central bankers said they stand in "full solidarity" with Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, an unprecedented step that underlines how seriously they view the threat to the independence of the U.S. institution.

The heads of the European Central Bank and its counterparts in the U.K., Sweden, Denmark, Switzerland, Australia, Canada, South Korea and Brazil issued a joint statement Tuesday. The signatories also include leaders from the Bank for International Settlements, a grouping of central banks.

"We stand in full solidarity with the Federal Reserve System and its Chair Jerome H. Powell," the statement said. "The independence of central banks is a cornerstone of price, financial and economic stability in the interest of the citizens that we serve."

TALKING POINT Wealthfront Stock Plunges After First Earnings Report Since IPO

Shares of Wealthfront dropped 14% Tuesday morning after the wealth management company reported quarterly earnings and provided data on Monday afternoon that showed some softening in asset flows in November and December.

The stock took a beating even as Wealthfront's quarterly earnings met Wall Street expectations. The company reported higher revenue and net income for the quarter ending Oct. 31 than the same period a year ago, thanks in part to a bull market for stocks that spurred customers to put more money in their investment accounts.

Quarterly net income came in at $30.9 million, up 3% year over year. Revenue was a record $93.2 million, up 16%.

Wealthfront also posted monthly statistics that showed December experienced outflows of $208 million. For December 2024, the company had $874 million in net flows. Executives said on the company's earnings call on Monday that recent interest-rate cuts spurred clients to reallocate some money.

"When interest rates decline, we expect to see a slowdown in cash management assets and an increase in investment advisory asset growth," CEO David Fortunato told analysts, according to a transcript provided by FactSet. "Ultimately, our focus is on continuing to grow total platform assets."

The company plans to do that by incentivizing clients to use more of Wealthfront's growing array of products and services. Some analysts acknowledged the bumps, but focused on the company's trajectory.

"Following its first earnings report after the December IPO, we think the long-term drivers of the Wealthfront platform remain intact, but we also acknowledge some near-term choppiness in flows given the evolving macro backdrop," J.P. Morgan Securities analyst Kenneth B. Worthington wrote in a Jan. 13 research note.

  • Andrew Welsch, Barron's

Expected Major Events for Wednesday

00:01/UK: Dec UK Nations and Regions Growth Tracker

06:00/JPN: Dec Preliminary Machine Tool Orders

12:00/US: 01/09 MBA Weekly Mortgage Applications Survey

13:30/US: Nov PPI

data.title: JPMorgan, Delta, Intel, AMD, Adobe, Moderna, Super Micro, and More Stock Market Movers — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni and George Glover

Stocks were falling Tuesday after the latest data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics showed inflation held steady in December as prices for goods and services rose 2.7%.

These stocks were making moves:

JPMorgan Chase fell 4%. The bank's profit declined 7% in the fourth quarter, even as revenue rose on the back of higher fees and interest income. Earnings of $4.63 a share fell short of analysts' calls for $4.85.

Delta Air Lines declined 2.8%. The legacy carrier beat Wall Street forecasts for adjusted earnings and revenue in the fourth quarter, but issued 2026 guidance that missed expectations. The airline called for 2026 earnings in the range of $6.50 to $7.50 a share, below analysts' calls for $7.28 at the midpoint.

Bank of New York Mellon rose 1.4%. Fourth-quarter adjusted earnings came in at $2.08 a share, topping the $1.99 a share analysts had expected, while total revenue climbed 7% to $5.18 billion, beating calls for $5.14 billion.

L3Harris Technologies pared back earlier gains to trade flat. The defense contractor said it plans to spin off its missile business into a separate public company. The Defense Department will invest $1 billion in the business through a convertible preferred security, which will convert to common equity upon an initial public offering later this year.

Intel climbed 8.8% to $47.93. KeyBanc analyst John Vinh upgraded shares to Overweight from Sector Weight with a $60 price target, pointing to strong demand from cloud service providers.

Advanced Micro Devices received a rating change for the same reason. Shares climbed 6.9% to $222.07 after Vinh upgraded the stock to Overweight from Sector Weight and assigned a $270 price target.

Adobe was down 5.5% after Apple announced it was launching a subscription plan for its creative software apps. The move places competitive pressure on Adobe, which is known for its editing apps like Photoshop and Premiere.

Moderna jumped 15%. Speaking at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference on Monday, CEO Stéphane Bancel said the company expects to roll out its combined flu and Covid vaccine over the next two years, with a 2027 launch in Europe following its approval this year and a U.S. launch in 2028.

Super Micro Computer declined 6.1% to $28.30. Goldman Sachs maintained a Sell rating and cut its price target on the stock to $26 from $34, citing the company's shrinking profit margin.

Revvity jumped 5.4% after the health-sciences company raised its full-year outlook ahead of earnings early next month.

Alphabet rose 1.6%. The Google parent's class A shares added 1% on Monday, closing at a record high of $331.86. The gains lifted the search-engine provider's market capitalization above $4 trillion for the first time. Alphabet became the fourth U.S. company to reach this milestone after Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft.

Cardinal Health advanced 3.2%. The healthcare services company said it expects fiscal-year earnings of at least $10 a share, up from a prior range of $9.65 to $9.85. Analysts tracked by FactSet were looking for $9.86 a share. The company also projected over $50 billion in specialty revenue for the fiscal year.

Travere Therapeutics sank 19% after the drugmaker received additional information requests from the Food and Drug Administration to characterize the clinical benefit of Filspari, its drug candidate for a rare kidney disorder.

AbbVie slipped 0.3%. The drug company reached a three-year agreement with the Trump administration to lower the prices of some drugs and invest in U.S. research and development in exchange for tariff exemption.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com and George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Clene Signs Multiple Financing Agreements
data.description:
Clene announced two financing agreements to support its lead therapy CNM-Au8 and corporate needs. The company entered a Securities Purchase Agreement to sell common stock and warrants at $6.50 per unit, targeting approximately $6.0 million in gross proceeds. The warrants are immediately exercisable at $6.00 per share, with potential exercise price increases tied to FDA-related announcements and stock price thresholds. Clene also appointed BTIG as exclusive placement agent on a best-efforts basis, with customary fees and expense reimbursements.

Agreement 1: Clene Signs Securities Purchase Agreement With Institutional Investors and Stockholders

  • Agreement type: Registered direct offering of common stock and warrants
  • Counterparty: Institutional Investors and Stockholders
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 08 2026 / Jan 13 2026
  • Reason: Raise capital to fund CNM-Au8 and corporate purposes

Agreement 2: Clene Signs Placement Agency Agreement With BTIG

  • Agreement type: Exclusive placement agency agreement (best-efforts)
  • Counterparty: BTIG
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 08 2026 / same
  • Reason: Facilitate equity financing to support development and operations

Original SEC Filing: Clene Inc. [ CLNN ] - 8-K - Jan. 13, 2026

data.title: Australia shares poised to open lower, NZ down
data.description:
Australian shares were expected to open lower on Monday, tracking a weak run by Wall Street stocks overnight, with subdued iron ore and copper prices likely to drag mining stocks.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) fell 0.1%, a 29.5-point discount to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark rose 0.6% on Tuesday.

Across the Tasman Sea, New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index fell 0.5% in early trade.

data.title: Salesforce and Adobe see their stocks slide as AI fears intensify
data.description:
By Christine Ji

The two software stocks were among the S&P 500's biggest losers on Tuesday as investors considered the threat AI poses to their business models

Both Adobe and Salesforce have shed more than 20% of their value in the last year.

Software stocks haven't been able to shake off the narrative of artificial-intelligence disruption. In fact, those concerns seem to be deepening.

Shares of Salesforce (CRM) and Adobe (ADBE) were hit hard on Tuesday, suffering some of the steepest losses in the S&P 500 SPX. Salesforce's stock was the biggest loser - down 6.5% on the day to record its largest percentage decline since May 30, 2024, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Adobe's stock slid 5.4%, ranking as Tuesday's third-worst daily performer on the S&P 500 and closing at its lowest level in more than three years.

Investors are concerned that productivity enhancements from AI will reduce demand for these traditional software giants' services. Additionally, the AI features that Salesforce and Adobe have rolled out are still in the early stages of adoption, leaving investors skeptical of their value. Adobe's stock has shed more than 24% over the last year, while Salesforce's stock has lost 23%.

David Wagner, head of equity and portfolio manager at Aptus Capital Advisors, characterized the negative sentiment as part of a "pair trade" where investors are rotating money out of software stocks to put into the more popular semiconductor space.

Read: Adobe and these 9 fellow tech stocks are rarely this cheap. Are they great deals or value traps?

Sentiment on Adobe continued to sour on Tuesday, as Oppenheimer analyst Brian Schwartz downgraded the stock to perform from outperform and removed his previous $430 price target. While Schwartz had previously been optimistic that Adobe's AI initiatives could reaccelerate growth in its Digital Media business, "this did not play out as expected" in 2025, he wrote.

A key concern for investors is Adobe's increased competition, spanning AI-native creative platforms such as Canva and Figma (FIG) to Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) Gemini's Nano Banana. Schwartz believes this "challenging operating environment" will make it difficult for Adobe to grow its top line.

While Schwartz was more optimistic about Salesforce, he caveated that it's still a "show-me stock," meaning that the company will need to firmly demonstrate that its Agentforce product is receiving steady adoption.

To dispel negative AI sentiment, software stocks "are going to have to execute over a few quarters" to convince investors of their long-term viability, Wagner told MarketWatch.

See more: Microsoft, Oracle and ServiceNow could be the top stocks to play a software comeback

-Christine Ji

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Broadcom to Sell $4.5 Billion of Notes to Buyers Led by JPMorgan, BofA
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Broadcom will sell $4.5 billion of senior notes to a group of underwriters led by Bank of America and JPMorgan.

The semiconductor manufacturer said Tuesday that it will use the proceeds from the sale for general corporate purposes and debt repayment.

The transaction includes $750 million worth of 4.3% interest rate notes due 2031, as well as $1.25 billion each of 4.6% notes due 2033, 4.95% notes due 2036, and 5.7% notes due 2056, the company said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Procter & Gamble Co Declares Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
On January 13, 2026, Procter & Gamble Co announced a quarterly dividend of $1.0568 per share.

Key Highlights:

  • Dividend payable on February 17, 2026.
  • Record date for shareholders is January 23, 2026.
  • P&G has paid dividends for 135 consecutive years.
  • Dividend increased for 69 consecutive years.
  • Commitment to return cash to shareholders emphasized.

Original SEC Filing: PROCTER & GAMBLE Co [ PG ] - 8-K - Jan. 13, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Myseum, Inc. Officially Launches Picture Party in North America
data.description:
On January 13, 2026, Myseum, Inc. announced the North American launch of its Picture Party app for iOS and Android.

Key Highlights:

  • Picture Party offers private, encrypted spaces for photo sharing.
  • The app addresses privacy and ownership issues in social media.
  • Worldwide rollout and new features planned in coming months.
  • Supported by a newly allowed U.S. patent for its core technology.
  • Available for download on iOS App Store and Google Play Store.

Original SEC Filing: Myseum, Inc. [ MYSE ] - 8-K - Jan. 13, 2026

data.description:

data.title: MAMA: Aggressive growth, margin expansion, and M&A drive ambitions for $1B revenue in deli-prepared foods
data.description:
A leading deli-prepared foods manufacturer is leveraging a rebuilt management team, operational improvements, and a flexible branded/private label strategy to drive growth in a fragmented $60B category. Expansion, innovation, and M&A are fueling ambitions to reach $1B in revenue and mid-teens EBITDA margin.
